name: Background job queue
description: Move slow retryable work outside request path
when_to_use: Email PDF media import export notifications and third-party sync
trade_offs: Needs worker monitoring retries and DLQ
implementation_notes: Return accepted response or domain state and process with idempotent worker
keywords: queue worker background job email pdf image

name: Transactional outbox
description: Persist domain event in same transaction as state change and publish later
when_to_use: Reliable event publication from relational databases
trade_offs: Adds outbox table publisher and cleanup process
implementation_notes: Write outbox row in transaction poll publish mark sent idempotently

name: Kafka event stream
description: Durable ordered event log for high-throughput streams
when_to_use: Audit logs analytics event sourcing cross-service data pipelines
trade_offs: Operational overhead and consumer lag management
implementation_notes: Partition by key manage schema compatibility and monitor lag

name: RabbitMQ work queue
description: Message broker for task distribution and routing
when_to_use: Background jobs notifications workflows and moderate event routing
trade_offs: Requires ack retry and DLQ discipline
implementation_notes: Use durable queues manual ack prefetch and dead-letter exchange

name: Idempotency key store
description: Deduplicate repeated externally-triggered writes
when_to_use: Payments webhooks retries imports and unreliable clients
trade_offs: Requires retention policy and response replay handling
implementation_notes: Store key request hash status and response for a bounded TTL

name: Webhook inbox
description: Persist incoming webhook before processing
when_to_use: Payment billing CRM and external integration callbacks
trade_offs: Adds storage and replay process
implementation_notes: Verify signature store raw event dedupe then process asynchronously

name: Dead letter queue
description: Separate messages that exceeded retries for inspection or replay
when_to_use: Any queue with retryable failures
trade_offs: DLQ can become ignored backlog without ownership
implementation_notes: Attach alerting ownership and replay tooling
keywords: dead letter queue retry failure worker

name: Durable scheduled jobs
description: Run recurring work through persistent scheduler state
when_to_use: Billing renewals reminders cleanup and reports
trade_offs: Clock and duplicate execution need handling
implementation_notes: Store schedule state and use idempotent execution locks
keywords: scheduled job cron durable billing reminder

name: Durable workflow engine
description: Coordinate long-running stateful processes
when_to_use: Onboarding fulfillment approvals and multi-step integrations
trade_offs: Adds platform dependency and modeling overhead
implementation_notes: Use when process spans minutes to days and needs retries or signals

name: Chunked batch processing
description: Process large imports exports or backfills in bounded chunks
when_to_use: CSV imports migrations reports and reindexing
trade_offs: Requires progress tracking and resume logic
implementation_notes: Use checkpoints batch size limits and idempotent item processing

name: Partitioned message ordering
description: Maintain order for events sharing the same key
when_to_use: Ledger events chat messages inventory item streams
trade_offs: Hot keys can limit throughput
implementation_notes: Partition by aggregate id and design consumers for per-key ordering

name: Notification fan-out queue
description: Split notification delivery by channel and recipient
when_to_use: Email push SMS in-app notification systems
trade_offs: Can amplify work and duplicate sends
implementation_notes: Create delivery records and dedupe by notification recipient channel
keywords: notification fanout email push sms queue

name: Idempotent consumer
description: Allow safe repeated message delivery
when_to_use: At-least-once queues Kafka RabbitMQ SQS and webhooks
trade_offs: Requires dedupe storage or natural idempotency
implementation_notes: Track processed message IDs or make updates conditional
keywords: idempotent consumer at least once duplicate message

name: Priority queues
description: Process urgent jobs before low-priority work
when_to_use: User-facing notifications payments and mixed background work
trade_offs: Can starve low-priority tasks
implementation_notes: Use separate queues workers and aging policy

name: Request reply over queue
description: Use queue for async processing with correlated response
when_to_use: Long-running tasks needing eventual response
trade_offs: Client complexity and timeout handling increase
implementation_notes: Return job id and expose status or callback

name: Claim check pattern
description: Send pointer to payload instead of large message
when_to_use: Large files reports and event payloads
trade_offs: Requires durable object storage and permissions
implementation_notes: Store payload in object storage and message only reference
keywords: claim check message payload object storage

name: Inbox outbox pair
description: Combine incoming dedupe with outgoing reliable publish
when_to_use: Integration services bridging external systems
trade_offs: More tables and cleanup required
implementation_notes: Use inbox for received events and outbox for emitted events

name: Event schema registry
description: Manage event compatibility across producers and consumers
when_to_use: Kafka streams shared event platforms and analytics
trade_offs: Requires governance
implementation_notes: Version schemas and enforce backward compatibility

name: Consumer group scaling
description: Scale event consumers horizontally with partitions
when_to_use: Kafka high-throughput event handling
trade_offs: Parallelism limited by partition count
implementation_notes: Choose partition key and count from throughput and ordering needs

name: Delayed retry queue
description: Retry failed jobs after delay without blocking workers
when_to_use: Email webhooks external APIs and transient failures
trade_offs: Retry delays can hide urgent failures
implementation_notes: Use exponential backoff queues and max attempts

name: Exactly-once effect
description: Achieve practical exactly-once through idempotent writes
when_to_use: At-least-once delivery systems with side effects
trade_offs: True exactly-once is rarely available end to end
implementation_notes: Use dedupe keys unique constraints and transactional writes
keywords: exactly once idempotent at least once

name: Transactional job enqueue
description: Enqueue job only if database transaction commits
when_to_use: Work triggered by domain state changes
trade_offs: Naive enqueue can run before commit
implementation_notes: Use outbox or transaction-aware enqueue hook

name: Progress tracking table
description: Track long job progress and resumability
when_to_use: Imports exports backfills and media processing
trade_offs: Adds state writes
implementation_notes: Store status percentage checkpoint error and timestamps

name: Cancellable job
description: Allow users or operators to cancel long work
when_to_use: Reports imports ML jobs and exports
trade_offs: Requires cooperative cancellation
implementation_notes: Check cancellation flag between chunks and persist canceled state

name: Rate-limited worker
description: Throttle calls to external APIs from workers
when_to_use: Third-party APIs email SMS and payment providers
trade_offs: Can increase queue latency
implementation_notes: Use token bucket per provider or tenant

name: Fan-in aggregator
description: Combine many events into one summarized result
when_to_use: Analytics notifications and workflow completion
trade_offs: Requires timeout and missing event handling
implementation_notes: Track expected events and emit aggregate when complete

name: Fan-out topic
description: Publish one event to multiple independent consumers
when_to_use: Domain events analytics email search and audit
trade_offs: Consumers need independent failure handling
implementation_notes: Use topic or stream with consumer groups

name: Worker heartbeat
description: Detect stuck jobs and reclaim safely
when_to_use: Long-running workers and distributed job systems
trade_offs: False positives can duplicate work
implementation_notes: Update heartbeat and reclaim only idempotent jobs

name: Job uniqueness
description: Prevent duplicate queued jobs for same resource
when_to_use: Exports imports sync and scheduled tasks
trade_offs: Uniqueness window can block legitimate reruns
implementation_notes: Use unique key with TTL and explicit override

name: Out-of-order event handling
description: Handle events that arrive out of sequence
when_to_use: Distributed event systems and replicated sources
trade_offs: Requires version or timestamp logic
implementation_notes: Store version and ignore stale events or rebuild projection
keywords: out of order event version projection
